user_message: Can you extract the deployment blockers from this escalation email for Woodgrove Labs? I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Woodgrove Labs
Date: 2024-03-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on deployment blockers from our last session.

[00:06:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:11:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:16:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:29:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-K trap (Follow-up email forwards internal language verbatim); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-K failure mode (Follow-up email forwards internal language verbatim)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-K -- Follow-up email forwards internal language verbatim
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
